title: cmd/skopeo/inspect: fix tag not being populated

comments.body:
Is this a bug at all? What needs the value, for what purpose? Quoting `types.go`:

``` go
// The Tag field is a legacy field which is here just for the Docker v2s1 manifest. It won't be supported
// for other manifest types.
```

In the old code, `skopeo inspect` fetches the `tag` value from the remote manifest; it is kind of weird piece of information to ask for, but it is a reasonable and logical that `skopeo inspect` would be a command to fetch data from the registry and return a value available in that data.

With this change, `skopeo inspect` just passes the provided transport`:`transport-specific reference through our parsing mechanisms, and then returns a part of the result of this parsing, completely independent of the remote repo.

I’m _perfectly fine_ with `skopeo` providing a “parse Docker reference to components and reformat as needed” functionality, but it seems weird to bundle this into the `skopeo inspect` “fetch a remote manifest and return information from that” subcommand. I was always thinking of the usage of “`skopeo inspect` and read `name` to expand a Docker reference” as a stopgap taking advantage of an existing weird case, until skopeo exposes that in a more structured way, not as a template to further expansion to `skopeo inspect`.

https://github.com/projectatomic/atomic/issues/578 suggested a new skopeo subcommand for expanding the reference, this seems broadly in the same category. Perhaps we need a `skopeo DRcalc` (by analogy with `ipcalc`), with `--tag`, `--fully-expanded`, `--canonical`, and so on.

In short, @baude asked for this because it seemed a bug to have tag exposed for V1 and for V2. While you're right V2 isn't carrying such it might be still OK to report this as part of inspect (in V2 case just as a parsed transport reference).

No, let’s not muddy `inspect` any more, and make it any more essential.

`skopeo docker-reference --tag $docker-reference`, or something like that, please.

I'm not sure about that, then let's remove this altogether and just use "docker/distribution" references.. That library should then be translated to python in some way. I don't like a command "docker-reference".

I did ask “What needs the value, for what purpose?”.  Are we considering changing it only for the (incorrectly perceived) symmetry, or does `atomic` or something else really need to compute the value?

(I would, very mildly, prefer a `skopeo docker-reference`, or `skopeo DRcalc`, or whatever, allowing `atomic` or anyone else to use the canonical `docker/docker/reference` implementation, to `atomic` reimplementing `docker/docker/reference` in Python, to avoid the risk of behavior drift. OTOH I can see that the overhead of extra subprocesses may be unattractive, and if, as I understand, `atomic` is already reimplementing that package in Python, then tag extraction is pretty much the most trivial aspect of that reimplementation.)
I have been working on a python replacement.  I'll begin to merge it into Atomic.
